commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e05fa8a1104f80e9a81b44671337d4c351f2cb59)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `cacheRetention: "standard"` to `"short"` normalization in the embedded agent cache-retention resolver for Anthropic-family paths and adds resolver tests for Bedrock, direct Anthropic, Vertex, Google, prompt-cache-key, and non-eligible providers.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +95. Total +101 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main's resolver only recognizes `none`, `short`, and `long`, so `cacheRetention: "standard"` on an `amazon-bedrock` Claude model falls through to `undefined` before provider options are built.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed for the config-contract choice and contributor real behavior proof; there is no safe automated repair lane until that direction is chosen.

target.title: fix(agents): map cacheRetention 'standard' to 'short' for Bedrock Claude models
target.body:
## Root Cause

`resolveCacheRetention()` in `prompt-cache-retention.ts` only recognizes `'none'`, `'short'`, and `'long'` via strict equality. When a user sets `cacheRetention: 'standard'` in their model params (a valid Bedrock cache retention value), the value falls through the check and returns `undefined` instead of resolving to `'short'`. The Bedrock provider's own default masks this by falling back to `'short'` when `cacheRetention` is `undefined`, making the bug silent — the user's explicit `'standard'` setting is effectively ignored without any warning.

## Summary

- Bug: `cacheRetention='standard'` (a valid Bedrock cache retention level) is silently ignored for Bedrock Claude models because `resolveCacheRetention()` doesn't recognize the value. The provider's fallback masks the issue, so users never see an error.
- Fix: Added a mapping of `'standard'` → `'short'` in `resolveCacheRetention()`, scoped to the `family` (Anthropic/Bedrock cache family) gate so Google and prompt-cache-key providers are not affected. Added regression tests verifying the alias does not leak outside its intended scope.
- Fixes #94482

## Revisions

This revision addresses ClawSweeper review feedback:
- [P2] Scoped `standard` → `short` alias to `family` (Anthropic/Bedrock cache family) only — Google and prompt-cache-key providers no longer accept the alias.
- [P0] Added real behavior proof with runtime test results and binary verification.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts` — 15/15 passed (7 new tests + 8 existing, including 2 regression tests for Google/prompt-cache-key)
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.cache-retention-default.test.ts` — 22/22 passed
- `pnpm check:test-types` — passed
- Autoreview: clean — no accepted/actionable findings
- Import cycles: `pnpm check:import-cycles` — clean
- No lock files changed
- Binary: OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (fb68f1e) matches HEAD

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: User's `cacheRetention='standard'` config in model params was silently ignored for Bedrock Claude models. This fix maps it to `'short'` only within the Anthropic/Bedrock cache family, and adds regression tests that Google and prompt-cache-key providers do not accept the alias.

Real environment tested: Linux (4.19.112), Node v24.13.1, branch `fix/issue-94482-bedrock-cacheretention-standard` at commit `fb68f1ea710e50091f79aa5e03a5af6864cde8f4`. Binary `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (fb68f1e)` confirmed matching HEAD.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
git diff src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.ts
pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts -- --reporter verbose
pnpm openclaw --version
```

Evidence after fix:
```diff
-  if (newVal === "standard") {
+  if (newVal === "standard" && family) {
     return "short";
   }
```
Test results (15/15 passed):
```
 ✓ maps 'standard' cacheRetention to 'short' for Anthropic Bedrock Claude models (issue #94482)
 ✓ maps 'standard' cacheRetention to 'short' for direct Anthropic models
 ✓ maps 'standard' cacheRetention to 'short' for anthropic-vertex models
 ✓ does not map 'standard' cacheRetention to 'short' for Google models       ← regression test
 ✓ does not map 'standard' cacheRetention to 'short' for prompt-cache-key providers  ← regression test
 ✓ does not map 'standard' cacheRetention for non-Anthropic Bedrock models
 ✓ does not map 'standard' cacheRetention for non-eligible providers
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
```
Binary verification: OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (fb68f1e) on branch `fix/issue-94482-bedrock-cacheretention-standard`, HEAD `fb68f1ea710e50091f79aa5e03a5af6864cde8f4` matching the committed fix.

Observed result after fix: `cacheRetention='standard'` correctly maps to `'short'` for all eligible Anthropic providers (Bedrock Claude, direct Anthropic, Anthropic Vertex). Google and prompt-cache-key providers correctly return `undefined` for the `'standard'` alias, confirming it does not leak beyond the intended Anthropic/Bedrock cache family.

What was not tested: Live E2E against a real Bedrock endpoint (requires AWS credentials). The fix is a purely additive string-mapping change in cache retention resolution logic with an additional scope gate, comprehensively covered by unit tests including explicit regression tests for non-target providers.
target.author: xialonglee
